mnemonics: INDEX: Investigate, Navigate, Determine, Execute, eXamine
keyPoints: The indexOf() method returns the first occurrence index of a substring.
The lastIndexOf() method returns the last occurrence index of a substring.
The contains() method returns a boolean if the substring exists.
The startsWith() and endsWith() methods check prefix and suffix.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that string indices start at 0, not 1.
Assuming contains() returns the index instead of a boolean.
Using == instead of .equals() for comparing string content after search.
Not handling the return value of -1 from indexOf() when a substring is not found.
